hero.title: Website & PDF Accessibility
hero.subtitle: The automated accessibility testing platform for development teams.
hero.subtitlePart2: scan websites and PDFs, fix WCAG violations fast, and generate
hero.description: scan websites and PDFs, fix WCAG violations fast, and generate {{reports}} for EU compliance audits.
hero.srWebScan: Navigate to the scan page to generate an accessibility improvement report for your website
hero.srPDFScan: Navigate to the PDF scan page to generate an accessibility improvement report for your PDF documents

seo.title: Increase traffic and optimize SEO
seo.description: Web accessibility isn't just about compliance—it's a powerful SEO strategy. Recent studies show that accessible websites significantly outperform their non-compliant competitors in search rankings.

study1.title: WCAG Compliant Sites Gain More Traffic
study1.description: A comprehensive study analyzing 10,000 websites found that WCAG compliant sites gained {{traffic}} more organic traffic and ranked for {{keywords}} more keywords. Meanwhile, non-compliant sites lost 20-30% of traffic to AI search tools, as AI agents need accessible structures to parse content effectively.

study2.title: Google's Algorithm Rewards Accessibility
study2.description: Research on Google's 2025 algorithm updates shows accessible websites can attract up to {{traffic}} more traffic from search engines. The overlap between accessibility features and Core Web Vitals means every accessibility improvement also strengthens your SEO foundation.

coreWebVitals.title: Better Core Web Vitals
coreWebVitals.description: Accessible code is cleaner and faster, improving LCP, FID, and CLS scores that Google uses for ranking.

aiSearch.title: AI Search Ready
aiSearch.description: AI agents parse sites like screen readers. Accessible structure means better visibility in AI-powered search.

bounceRates.title: Lower Bounce Rates
bounceRates.description: Accessible sites remove barriers that cause visitors to leave, sending positive engagement signals to Google.

features.title: Everything your team needs to ship accessible products
features.description: From automated WCAG scanning to workflow integrations, LumTrails streamlines accessibility testing for modern development teams.

webScanning.title: Multi-Engine Web Scanning
webScanning.description: Comprehensive WCAG 2.1 testing with axe-core, Nu HTML Checker, axtree analysis, Galen layout testing, and link validation—all in one scan.

pdfAnalysis.title: AI-Powered PDF Analysis
pdfAnalysis.description: Advanced PDF accessibility scanning using GPT vision analysis. Detect missing alt text, reading order issues, and document structure problems.

api.title: Developer-First API
api.description: RESTful API for seamless CI/CD integration. Automate accessibility testing in your deployment pipeline and fix violations directly from scan results.

integrations.title: Workflow Integrations
integrations.description: Push scan results directly to GitHub Issues, Slack channels, or Notion pages. Keep your team informed without leaving your existing tools.

euCompliance.title: EU Accessibility Act Ready
euCompliance.description: Generate Accessibility Improvement Reports and iterate towards European Accessibility Act compliance with actionable insights.

progressTracking.title: Progress Tracking
progressTracking.description: Compare scans over time to measure your accessibility improvements. Show stakeholders concrete progress with historical data.

legal.title: Stay ahead of accessibility regulations
legal.description: The European Accessibility Act is now in effect. Proactive accessibility testing helps you avoid fines up to {{fine}} and build better products for everyone.

eaa2025.title: European Accessibility Act 2025
eaa2025.description: Since June 2025, websites and digital products must be accessible. LumTrails helps development teams quickly identify and fix WCAG violations before audits.

improvement.title: Demonstrate Continuous Improvement
improvement.description: Regular accessibility scans and improvement reports show auditors your ongoing commitment to compliance—even while working toward full conformance.

wcag.title: WCAG 2.1 Level AA Coverage
wcag.description: Our multi-engine scanning covers WCAG success criteria across perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust principles with actionable fix recommendations.

developer.title: Built for Developer Workflows
developer.description: API-first design means you can integrate accessibility testing into CI/CD pipelines, automate issue creation, and track fixes across sprints.

integrations.title: Integrate with your favorite tools
integrations.description: Automatically push accessibility scan results from both web and PDF scans directly into your existing workflows. No manual copy-pasting required.

github.name: GitHub
github.description: Automatically create GitHub Issues when accessibility problems are found. Track and assign fixes directly in your development workflow.

slack.name: Slack
slack.description: Get instant notifications in your Slack channels. Keep your team informed about accessibility issues as soon as they are detected.

notion.name: Notion
notion.description: Automatically create pages in your Notion workspace with detailed scan results. Organize accessibility documentation effortlessly.

cta.title: Ready to automate your accessibility testing?
cta.description: Start with a free scan, explore the API, or upgrade for unlimited scans and workflow integrations.

accessibility.reviewNote: This statement was created using self-evaluation with the tools and methods described above. We regularly review and update this statement to reflect changes to our website.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: LumTrails ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring the security of your personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your information when you use our website accessibility scanning platform at your-domain.com.
privacy.introGdpr: This policy is designed to comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) (EU) 2016/679 and other applicable data protection laws.

dataController.title: 1. Data Controller
dataController.intro: The data controller responsible for your personal data is:

personalData.title: 2. Personal Data We Collect
personalData.intro: We collect and process the following categories of personal data:
usageItems: API usage logs (if using our API)
integrationItems: GitHub, Notion, or Slack connection tokens (encrypted)

legalBasis.title: 3. Legal Basis for Processing
legalBasis.intro: We process your personal data based on the following legal grounds under GDPR:

purposes.title: 4. Purposes of Processing
purposes.intro: We use your personal data for the following purposes:
items: Service Delivery: To provide website and PDF accessibility scanning services
Account Management: To create and manage your user account
Billing: To process payments and manage subscriptions through Stripe
Communication: To send service-related notifications and respond to your inquiries
Integrations: To connect your account with third-party services (GitHub, Notion, Slack) when authorized
Improvement: To analyze usage patterns and improve our services
Security: To protect against unauthorized access and ensure platform security
Legal Compliance: To comply with applicable laws and regulations

retention.title: 5. Data Retention
retention.intro: We retain your personal data for the following periods:
items: Account data: Until you delete your account, plus 30 days for backup recovery
Scan results: Retention period depends on your subscription plan - Free plan: 30 days, Standard plan: 6 months, Business plan: 1 year from the scan date, or until account deletion
Billing records: 10 years as required by French tax law
Technical logs: 90 days for security and debugging purposes
Integration tokens: Until you disconnect the integration or delete your account

processors.title: 6. Data Processors and Third Parties
processors.intro: We use the following third-party service providers (data processors) to deliver our services:
processors.note: All processors are bound by Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) and appropriate safeguards are in place for international transfers.

transfers.title: 7. International Data Transfers
transfers.intro: Some of our data processors are located outside the European Economic Area (EEA). When we transfer personal data outside the EEA, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place:
items: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs): EU-approved contractual terms for data transfers
Adequacy Decisions: For transfers to countries with adequate data protection levels
Additional Technical Measures: Encryption and access controls

rights.title: 8. Your Rights Under GDPR
rights.intro: Under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the following rights:
items: Right of Access (Art. 15): Request a copy of your personal data
Right to Rectification (Art. 16): Request correction of inaccurate data
Right to Erasure (Art. 17): Request deletion of your data ("right to be forgotten")
Right to Restriction (Art. 18): Request limitation of processing
Right to Data Portability (Art. 20): Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
Right to Object (Art. 21): Object to processing based on legitimate interests
Right to Withdraw Consent: Withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent
rights.exerciseRights: To exercise these rights, please contact us at hello@your-domain.com. We will respond within one month of receiving your request.

security.title: 9. Data Security
security.intro: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data, including:
items: Encryption of data in transit (TLS/SSL) and at rest
Secure authentication using Firebase Authentication
Access controls and principle of least privilege
Regular security assessments and updates
Encrypted storage of sensitive credentials and tokens
Hosting on Google Cloud Platform with enterprise-grade security

cookies.title: 10. Cookies
cookies.content: We use strictly necessary cookies for authentication and session management. For more details, please see our Cookie Policy.

complaints.title: 11. Complaints
complaints.intro: If you believe we have not handled your personal data properly, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the French data protection authority:
complaints.authority: Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés (CNIL)

changes.title: 12. Changes to This Policy
changes.content1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or legal requirements. We will notify you of any material changes by posting the updated policy on our website and updating the "Last Updated" date.
changes.content2: We encourage you to review this policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect your data.

contact.title: 13. Contact Us
contact.intro: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us:

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content1: By accessing or using the LumTrails website and services at your-domain.com (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, you may not access or use the Service.
acceptance.content2: These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and YOUR_COMPANY_NAME, operating as LumTrails (SIRET: YOUR_SIRET_NUMBER), located at YOUR_STREET_ADDRESS, YOUR_CITY_ZIP, YOUR_COUNTRY ("we", "us", "our").

description.title: 2. Description of Service
description.intro: LumTrails provides automated accessibility scanning services for websites and PDF documents. Our Service includes:
items: Web Accessibility Scanning: Automated analysis of websites using industry-standard tools including axe-core, Nu HTML Checker, Linkinator, Galen Framework, and axtree
PDF Accessibility Scanning: AI-powered analysis of PDF documents for accessibility compliance
Accessibility Improvement Reports: Detailed reports identifying accessibility issues and recommendations
Third-party Integrations: Integration with GitHub, Notion, and Slack for automated reporting
API Access: Programmatic access to our scanning services
description.disclaimer: Important Disclaimer: LumTrails provides Accessibility Improvement Reports, not compliance certifications. Our automated scans identify potential accessibility issues but cannot guarantee full compliance with WCAG, the EU Accessibility Act (EAA), or any other accessibility standards. Complete accessibility compliance requires manual testing, expert review, user testing with people with disabilities, and ongoing monitoring.

registration.title: 3. Account Registration
registration.intro: To access certain features of the Service, you must create an account. When creating an account, you agree to:
items: Provide accurate, current, and complete information
Maintain and promptly update your account information
Maintain the security and confidentiality of your login credentials
Accept responsibility for all activities that occur under your account
Notify us immediately of any unauthorized access to your account
registration.suspension: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms or engage in suspicious activity.

creditSystem.title: 4.1 Credit System
creditSystem.intro: Our Service operates on a credit-based system:
items: Web Scan Credits: 1 credit per scan module used
PDF Scan Credits: 1 credit per page analyzed

plans.title: 4.2 Subscription Plans
plans.content: We offer various subscription plans with different credit allocations. Details of current plans and pricing are available on our Pricing page.

renewal.title: 4.3 Credit Renewal
renewal.content: For paid plans, credits are allocated monthly and renew at the beginning of each billing cycle. Free plan users receive weekly credits that renew every Monday at 00:00 CET. Unused credits do not roll over to the following period.

billing.title: 4.4 Billing
billing.content: Payments are processed securely through Stripe. By subscribing to a paid plan, you authorize us to charge your payment method on a recurring basis until you cancel your subscription.

cancellation.title: 5. Cancellation and Refunds
cancellation.cancellationContent: You may cancel your subscription at any time through your account settings or by contacting us. Upon cancellation, you will retain access to your current plan's features until the end of your billing period.
cancellation.refundIntro: In accordance with French consumer protection law (Code de la consommation):
refundItems: For digital services, the right of withdrawal expires once the service has been fully performed with your prior express consent
By using scan credits, you acknowledge that the service has been performed and waive your right of withdrawal for those credits
cancellation.refundContact: To request a refund, please contact us at hello@your-domain.com.

acceptableUse.title: 6. Acceptable Use
acceptableUse.intro: You agree to use the Service only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree not to:
items: Use the Service to scan websites or documents you do not have authorization to access or test
Use the Service in any way that could damage, disable, or impair the Service
Attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Service or its systems
Use automated scripts or bots to access the Service outside of our official API
Reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to extract source code from the Service
Use the Service for any illegal or unauthorized purpose
Transmit malware, viruses, or other harmful code
Resell or redistribute the Service without our written permission
Use the Service in a manner that infringes upon the rights of others

ourIp.title: 7.1 Our Intellectual Property
ourIp.content: The Service, including its original content, features, functionality, design, and branding (excluding user-provided content), is and will remain the exclusive property of YOUR_COMPANY_NAME and is protected by French and international intellectual property laws.

yourContent.title: 7.2 Your Content
yourContent.content: You retain ownership of any URLs, PDFs, or other content you submit for scanning. By using the Service, you grant us a limited license to process this content solely for the purpose of providing the Service.

scanResults.title: 7.3 Scan Results
scanResults.content: The scan results and reports generated for your content are provided for your use. You may use, share, and incorporate these reports into your accessibility improvement documentation.

availability.title: 8. Service Availability and Modifications
availability.availabilityContent: We strive to maintain high availability of the Service but do not guarantee uninterrupted access. The Service may be temporarily unavailable due to maintenance, updates, or circumstances beyond our control.
availability.modificationsContent: We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any aspect of the Service at any time. We will provide reasonable notice of significant changes that may affect your use of the Service.

liability.title: 9. Limitation of Liability
liability.intro: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law:
items: The Service is provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, express or implied
We do not warrant that the Service will be error-free, secure, or uninterrupted
We do not guarantee that our scans will detect all accessibility issues
We are not liable for any damages arising from your use of or inability to use the Service
Our total liability shall not exceed the amount you paid for the Service in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim
liability.note: These limitations do not affect your statutory rights as a consumer under French or EU law.

indemnification.title: 10. Indemnification
indemnification.content: You agree to indemnify and hold harmless YOUR_COMPANY_NAME, LumTrails, and its affiliates from any claims, damages, losses, or expenses arising from your use of the Service, your violation of these Terms, or your infringement of any third-party rights.

privacy.title: 11. Privacy and Data Protection
privacy.content1: Your privacy is important to us. Our collection and use of personal data is governed by our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into these Terms by reference.
privacy.content2: By using the Service, you consent to the collection and use of your data as described in the Privacy Policy.

thirdParty.title: 12. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.content: The Service may integrate with third-party services (GitHub, Notion, Slack). Your use of these integrations is subject to the respective third-party terms and privacy policies. We are not responsible for the practices of third-party services.

termination.title: 13. Termination
termination.content1: We may terminate or suspend your account and access to the Service immediately, without prior notice, if you breach these Terms or engage in activities that may harm the Service or other users.
termination.content2: Upon termination, your right to use the Service will immediately cease. Sections of these Terms that by their nature should survive termination will remain in effect.

changes.title: 14. Changes to Terms
changes.content1: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. We will notify you of material changes by posting the updated Terms on the Service and updating the "Last Updated" date.
changes.content2: Your continued use of the Service after changes become effective constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms.

governing.title: 15. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
governing.content1: These Terms are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of France, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.
governing.content2: Any disputes arising under these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of YOUR_CITY, YOUR_COUNTRY.
governing.content3: For consumers residing in the European Union, you may also use the European Commission's Online Dispute Resolution platform:

severability.title: 16. Severability
severability.content: If any provision of these Terms is found to be unenforceable or invalid, that provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

contact.title: 17. Contact Information
contact.intro: For any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us:

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This Cookie Policy explains how LumTrails uses cookies and similar technologies when you visit our website at your-domain.com. This policy complies with the EU ePrivacy Directive (2002/58/EC) and the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

whatAreCookies.title: What Are Cookies?
whatAreCookies.content1: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile phone) when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites work more efficiently and to provide information to website owners.
whatAreCookies.content2: Cookies can be "persistent" (remaining on your device until they expire or are deleted) or "session" cookies (deleted when you close your browser).

cookiesWeUse.title: Cookies We Use
cookiesWeUse.intro: LumTrails uses only strictly necessary cookies that are essential for the operation of our website. We do not use any marketing, advertising, or non-essential tracking cookies.

strictlyNecessary.title: Strictly Necessary Cookies
strictlyNecessary.description: These cookies are essential for you to browse the website and use its features. Without these cookies, services you have asked for cannot be provided.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.content1: We use Firebase Authentication provided by Google for secure user login. Firebase may set cookies necessary for the authentication process. These are classified as strictly necessary cookies as they are essential for the service to function.
thirdParty.content2: If you use our payment features, Stripe (our payment processor) may set cookies necessary for secure payment processing.
items: Google Analytics or other analytics cookies
Advertising or marketing cookies
Social media tracking pixels
Session recording tools
A/B testing cookies

consent.title: Cookie Consent
consent.content1: Since LumTrails only uses strictly necessary cookies that are essential for the website to function, we are not required to obtain your consent under the ePrivacy Directive. These cookies cannot be disabled as they are necessary to provide the service.
consent.content2: If we ever decide to use non-essential cookies in the future (such as analytics), we will implement a proper cookie consent mechanism and update this policy accordingly.

managing.title: Managing Cookies
managing.intro: Most web browsers allow you to control cookies through their settings. You can:
items: View what cookies are stored on your device
Delete all or specific cookies
Block cookies from specific websites
Block all cookies (note: this may affect website functionality)
managing.browserLinks: Here are links to cookie management instructions for popular browsers:
managing.note: Please note that if you block or delete strictly necessary cookies, you may not be able to use certain features of our website, particularly those requiring authentication.

localStorage.title: Local Storage
localStorage.intro: In addition to cookies, we use your browser's local storage to store preferences such as:
items: Theme preference (dark/light mode)
UI state preferences
localStorage.note: Local storage data remains on your device and is not transmitted to our servers.

updates.title: Updates to This Policy
updates.content: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: If you have questions about our use of cookies, please contact us:

legalNotice.title: Legal Notice
legalNotice.intro: In accordance with the provisions of French Law No. 2004-575 of 21 June 2004 for Confidence in the Digital Economy (LCEN), EU E-Commerce Directive 2000/31/EC Article 5, and applicable French regulations, the following information is provided to users of the website your-domain.com.

publisher.title: 1. Website Publisher
rows: Not applicable (Micro-entreprise exempt from VAT – Article 293 B of the French Tax Code)

purpose.title: 3. Website Purpose
purpose.intro: LumTrails provides automated accessibility scanning services for websites and PDF documents. Our platform generates Accessibility Improvement Reports to help users identify and track progress toward compliance with:
items: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA
EU Web Accessibility Directive (2016/2102)
European Accessibility Act (EAA)
purpose.disclaimer: Important: LumTrails provides Accessibility Improvement Reports, not compliance certifications. Our automated scans help identify areas for improvement but do not guarantee full accessibility compliance. Complete compliance requires manual testing, expert review, and continuous improvement efforts.

ip.title: 4. Intellectual Property
ip.content1: The entire content of this website, including but not limited to text, graphics, logos, images, software, and the overall structure, is the exclusive property of YOUR_COMPANY_NAME or is used with the permission of the respective rights holders.
ip.content2: Any reproduction, representation, modification, publication, or adaptation of all or part of the elements of the site, regardless of the means or process used, is prohibited without prior written authorization from YOUR_COMPANY_NAME.
ip.content3: The LumTrails name and logo are trademarks. Any unauthorized use may constitute infringement under French intellectual property law (Code de la propriété intellectuelle).

personalData.title: 5. Personal Data
personalData.content1: The processing of personal data on this website is carried out in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and French Law No. 78-17 of 6 January 1978 (Loi Informatique et Libertés).
personalData.content2: For complete information about how we collect, use, and protect your personal data, please consult our Privacy Policy.

cookiesSection.title: 6. Cookies
cookiesSection.content: This website uses strictly necessary cookies for its operation. For more information, please consult our Cookie Policy.

applicableLaw.title: 7. Applicable Law
applicableLaw.content1: These legal notices are governed by French law. Any dispute arising from the use of this website shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of YOUR_CITY, YOUR_COUNTRY.
applicableLaw.content2: For consumers residing in the European Union, the European Commission provides an online dispute resolution platform:

contact.title: 8. Contact
contact.intro: For any questions or concerns regarding these legal notices, please contact us:
contact.contactFormLink: You can also use our Contact Form to reach us.

accessibilityStatement.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibilityStatement.subtitle: Our commitment to digital accessibility
accessibilityStatement.intro: LumTrails is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We are continually improving the user experience for everyone and applying the relevant accessibility standards.
accessibilityStatement.introApplies: This accessibility statement applies to the LumTrails website at your-domain.com.

conformance.title: Conformance Status
conformance.badge: Striving for WCAG 2.1 Level AA Conformance
conformance.badgeDescription: LumTrails aims to conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards through automated and manual testing.
conformance.content1: The Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) define requirements for designers and developers to improve accessibility for people with disabilities. It defines three levels of conformance: Level A, Level AA, and Level AAA.
conformance.content2: LumTrails strives to conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA. We have conducted comprehensive automated and manual accessibility testing and believe the website meets these standards. However, accessibility is an ongoing process and we continuously work to improve.

testing.title: Testing Methodology
testing.intro: LumTrails was tested using the following tools and methods:

automated.title: Automated Testing Tools
items: axe-core: Industry-standard accessibility testing engine for WCAG 2.1 violations
Nu HTML Checker: W3C markup validation service for HTML conformance
Linkinator: Link health validation for broken link detection
Galen Framework: Layout and responsive design testing
Accessibility Tree Analysis: Screen reader compatibility verification via Playwright

manual.title: Manual Testing
items: Keyboard-only navigation testing
Screen reader testing (NVDA, VoiceOver)
Color contrast verification
Text resize and zoom testing
Focus management verification

browsers.title: Browsers and Assistive Technologies Tested
items: Chrome with NVDA on Windows
Safari with VoiceOver on macOS
Firefox with NVDA on Windows
Chrome on Android with TalkBack
Safari on iOS with VoiceOver

technical.title: Technical Specifications
technical.intro: Accessibility of LumTrails relies on the following technologies:
items: HTML5
WAI-ARIA 1.2
CSS3
JavaScript (React)
technical.note: These technologies are relied upon for conformance with the accessibility standards used.

features.title: Accessibility Features
features.intro: LumTrails includes the following accessibility features:
items: Skip to main content: Allows keyboard users to bypass navigation
Dark mode: Reduces eye strain and improves readability in low-light conditions
Semantic HTML: Proper heading hierarchy and landmark regions for screen readers
Keyboard navigation: All interactive elements are accessible via keyboard
Focus indicators: Clear, visible focus states on all interactive elements
Form labels: All form inputs have associated labels
Error messages: Form errors are clearly identified with helpful suggestions
Responsive design: Content adapts to different screen sizes and zoom levels
Status announcements: Toast notifications use ARIA live regions

wcagDetails.title: WCAG 2.1 Level AA Compliance Details
wcagDetails.intro: The following table details our conformance with each WCAG 2.1 Level A and AA success criterion.

knownIssues.title: Known Accessibility Issues
knownIssues.content: We are not currently aware of any significant accessibility barriers on the LumTrails website. If you encounter any issues, please let us know.

feedback.title: Feedback and Contact
feedback.intro: We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of LumTrails. Please let us know if you encounter accessibility barriers:
feedback.responseTime: We aim to respond to accessibility feedback within 5 business days.

enforcement.title: Enforcement Procedure
enforcement.content1: If you are not satisfied with our response to your accessibility concern, you may contact the French Defender of Rights (Défenseur des droits):

dpa.title: Data Processing Agreement
dpa.intro: This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") forms part of the Terms of Service between LumTrails (operated by YOUR_COMPANY_NAME) and the Customer for the provision of accessibility scanning services.

definitions.title: 1. Definitions
items: "Controller" means the entity that determines the purposes and means of processing Personal Data (the Customer).
"Processor" means the entity that processes Personal Data on behalf of the Controller (LumTrails).
"Personal Data" means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person.
"Processing" means any operation performed on Personal Data, whether or not by automated means.
"Sub-processor" means any processor engaged by the Processor to process Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.
"Data Subject" means the individual to whom Personal Data relates.
"GDPR" means the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679.
"Services" means the accessibility scanning services provided by LumTrails.

scope.title: 2. Scope and Purpose
scope.intro: This DPA applies to the processing of Personal Data by LumTrails (Processor) on behalf of the Customer (Controller) in connection with the provision of the Services.

subjectMatter.title: 2.1 Subject Matter
subjectMatter.content: The subject matter of processing is the provision of automated accessibility scanning services for websites and PDF documents.

nature.title: 2.2 Nature and Purpose of Processing
nature.intro: LumTrails processes Personal Data for the following purposes:
items: Performing accessibility scans on Customer-provided URLs and documents
Generating and storing accessibility reports
Managing Customer accounts and authentication
Processing billing and subscription information
Delivering scan results to integrated third-party platforms when authorized

categories.title: 2.3 Categories of Data Subjects
items: Customer employees and authorized users
Individuals whose data may appear in scanned URLs or documents

types.title: 2.4 Types of Personal Data
items: Account information (email address, name)
Authentication data
Usage data and scan history
Content of scanned URLs and PDF documents (which may contain Personal Data)

lawful.title: 3.1 Lawful Processing
items: Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller
Immediately inform the Controller if we believe an instruction infringes GDPR
Process Personal Data only as necessary to provide the Services

confidentiality.title: 3.2 Confidentiality
items: Ensure that persons authorized to process Personal Data have committed to confidentiality
Limit access to Personal Data to personnel who require access to fulfill the Services

security.title: 3.3 Security Measures
security.intro: Implement appropriate technical and organizational measures including:
items: Encryption of Personal Data in transit and at rest
Access controls and authentication requirements
Regular security assessments and testing
Incident detection and response procedures
Data backup and recovery capabilities

subprocessors.title: 3.4 Sub-processors
items: Not engage another processor without prior written authorization from the Controller
Maintain a list of approved Sub-processors (see Section 5)
Ensure Sub-processors are bound by equivalent data protection obligations
Notify the Controller of any intended changes to Sub-processors

assistance.title: 3.5 Assistance to Controller
items: Assist the Controller in responding to Data Subject rights requests
Assist with data protection impact assessments where required
Assist with prior consultations with supervisory authorities
Make available all information necessary to demonstrate compliance

rights.title: 4. Data Subject Rights
rights.intro: LumTrails shall assist the Controller in fulfilling its obligations to respond to Data Subject requests exercising their rights under GDPR, including:
items: Right of Access (Art. 15): Providing access to Personal Data
Right to Rectification (Art. 16): Correcting inaccurate data
Right to Erasure (Art. 17): Deleting Personal Data upon request
Right to Restriction (Art. 18): Restricting processing as required
Right to Data Portability (Art. 20): Exporting data in machine-readable format
Right to Object (Art. 21): Ceasing processing upon objection
rights.response: The Controller shall submit Data Subject requests to hello@your-domain.com. LumTrails shall respond within 72 hours of receiving such requests.

subprocessorsList.title: 5. Sub-processors
subprocessorsList.intro: The Controller hereby authorizes LumTrails to engage the following Sub-processors:
subprocessorsList.notification: LumTrails will notify the Controller of any intended additions or replacements to this list at least 30 days in advance, allowing the Controller to object to such changes.

transfers.title: 6. International Data Transfers
transfers.intro: Where Personal Data is transferred outside the European Economic Area (EEA), LumTrails ensures appropriate safeguards are in place:
items: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs): EU-approved contractual clauses for international transfers
Supplementary Measures: Additional technical and organizational measures as recommended by the EDPB
Transfer Impact Assessments: Evaluation of third-country legal frameworks

notification.title: 7.1 Notification
notification.content: LumTrails shall notify the Controller without undue delay (and in any event within 72 hours) after becoming aware of a Personal Data breach.

content.title: 7.2 Breach Notification Content
items: Description of the nature of the breach
Categories and approximate number of Data Subjects affected
Categories and approximate number of Personal Data records affected
Name and contact details of the data protection point of contact
Likely consequences of the breach
Measures taken or proposed to address the breach

cooperation.title: 7.3 Cooperation
cooperation.content: LumTrails shall cooperate with the Controller and take reasonable steps to assist in the investigation, mitigation, and remediation of any security incident.

audit.title: 8. Audit Rights
audit.intro: LumTrails shall make available to the Controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA and allow for and contribute to audits and inspections conducted by the Controller or an auditor mandated by the Controller.
items: Conducted with reasonable advance notice (minimum 30 days)
Limited to once per year unless required by a supervisory authority
Conducted during normal business hours
Subject to reasonable confidentiality obligations

retentionPeriod.title: 9.1 Retention Period
retentionPeriod.content: LumTrails retains Personal Data for the duration of the Services agreement and for the period necessary to comply with legal obligations (see our Privacy Policy for specific retention periods).

deletion.title: 9.2 Deletion Upon Termination
deletion.intro: Upon termination of the Services agreement, LumTrails shall, at the Controller's choice:
items: Delete all Personal Data (and confirm deletion in writing), or
Return all Personal Data to the Controller in a structured, commonly used format
deletion.note: This obligation does not apply to the extent LumTrails is required to retain Personal Data under applicable law.

liability.title: 10. Liability
liability.content1: Each party's liability under this DPA is subject to the limitations and exclusions of liability set out in the Terms of Service.
liability.content2: Nothing in this DPA shall limit either party's liability for breaches of data protection law or for damages suffered by Data Subjects as a result of a breach of this DPA.

term.title: 11. Term and Termination
term.content1: This DPA shall remain in effect for the duration of the Services agreement and shall automatically terminate upon termination of the Services.
term.content2: Sections relating to data deletion, audit rights, and liability shall survive termination of this DPA.

governing.title: 12. Governing Law
governing.content1: This DPA is governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of France.
governing.content2: The courts of YOUR_CITY, YOUR_COUNTRY shall have exclusive jurisdiction to settle any disputes arising from this DPA.

contact.title: 13. Contact Information
contact.intro: For questions regarding this DPA or data processing matters:

dpa.subtitle: GDPR Article 28 Compliant Agreement

signedCopy.title: Need a Signed Copy?
signedCopy.description: If you require a countersigned copy of this DPA for your records or compliance purposes, please contact us and we will provide a signed PDF version.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Find answers to common questions about using LumTrails

getMore.question: How do I get more credits?
getMore.answer: You can get more credits by upgrading to a higher subscription plan. Visit the Pricing page to compare plans and their credit allocations. Standard plan includes 1,000 web scan credits and 50 PDF scan credits per month. Business plan includes 10,000 web scan credits and 500 PDF scan credits per month.

monitor.question: Where can I monitor my credit consumption?
monitor.answer: You can monitor your credit consumption by navigating to your Profile and selecting the "Usage History" tab. This section shows detailed statistics about your web and PDF scan usage over time, including monthly breakdowns and trends.

invoice.question: Where can I get my invoice?
invoice.answer: You can access your invoices by going to your Profile and selecting the "Invoices" tab. All your past purchases and subscription invoices are available for download there. Click on any invoice to view or download the PDF version.

pastScans.question: Where can I see my past scans?
pastScans.answer: You can view all your past scans in the "My Scans" section, accessible from the sidebar. This page displays both your website scans and PDF scans with their results, dates, and status.

exportScan.question: How can I export a scan?
exportScan.answer: There are two ways to export your scan results as a PDF: 1) When viewing a scan, click the "Download Report" button at the top of the results page. 2) In the "My Scans" section, click the "PDF" button next to any scan to download the accessibility report directly.

overview.question: What are Integrations?
overview.answer: Integrations allow you to automatically push your accessibility scan results to external tools like GitHub, Notion, and Slack. This helps your team stay informed about accessibility issues without leaving their existing workflows.

access.question: How do I access Integrations?
access.answer: Navigate to the "Integrations" section from the sidebar. Note that Integrations are available only for users with an active paid subscription (Standard or Business plan). Free plan users will see a prompt to upgrade.

connect.question: How do I connect GitHub?
connect.answer: Click the "Connect" button on the GitHub card. You will be redirected to GitHub to authorize LumTrails. Once authorized, you'll be returned to the Integrations page with your GitHub account connected.

configure.question: How do I configure GitHub Integration?
configure.answer: After connecting, click "Advanced Configuration" on the GitHub card. You can: 1) Select a repository where issues will be created. 2) Enable/disable automatic issue creation for web scans and PDF scans separately. 3) Configure severity filters to only create issues for certain violation levels. 4) Choose how violations are grouped into issues.

sections.question: What scan sections can I send to GitHub?
sections.answer: You can configure which scan sections create GitHub issues: WCAG Compliance, HTML Validation, Broken Links, Accessibility Tree, and Layout Issues. Each section can be enabled or disabled independently, and you can choose between creating multiple issues or grouping everything into a single issue.

disconnect.question: How do I disconnect GitHub?
disconnect.answer: In the GitHub configuration panel, scroll down to "Account Management" and click "Disconnect GitHub". This will remove all authorization tokens and reset your configuration. You can reconnect at any time.

connect.question: How do I connect Notion?
connect.answer: Click the "Connect" button on the Notion card. You will be redirected to Notion to authorize LumTrails and select which pages to share. Make sure to grant access to the parent page where you want scan results to be created.

configure.question: How do I configure Notion Integration?
configure.answer: After connecting, click "Advanced Configuration" on the Notion card. You must provide a Parent Page URL where new pages with scan results will be created as children. Enable/disable web scan and PDF scan push separately, and configure severity filters and grouping options.

parentPage.question: What is the Parent Page URL?
parentPage.answer: The Parent Page URL is the Notion page where scan result pages will be created as children. Copy the URL from your Notion workspace (it looks like https://www.notion.so/workspace/page-title-xxx). Make sure you've granted LumTrails access to this page during the OAuth authorization step.

disconnect.question: How do I disconnect Notion?
disconnect.answer: In the Notion configuration panel, scroll down to "Account Management" and click "Disconnect Notion". This removes all authorization tokens. You can reconnect at any time by re-authorizing.

connect.question: How do I connect Slack?
connect.answer: Click the "Connect" button on the Slack card. You'll need to provide a Webhook URL. To get this: 1) Go to api.slack.com/apps. 2) Create or select an app. 3) Enable "Incoming Webhooks". 4) Add a new webhook to your workspace. 5) Copy the webhook URL and paste it in LumTrails.

configure.question: How do I configure Slack Integration?
configure.answer: After connecting, click "Advanced Configuration" on the Slack card. You can: 1) Optionally override the default channel. 2) Enable/disable notifications for web scans and PDF scans. 3) Configure severity filters to only receive alerts for certain violation levels. 4) Choose how violations are grouped in messages.

channel.question: Can I send notifications to different channels?
channel.answer: Yes! You can optionally specify a channel override (e.g., #accessibility or @username) in the configuration. If not specified, notifications will be sent to the default channel configured in your Slack webhook.

disconnect.question: How do I disconnect Slack?
disconnect.answer: In the Slack configuration panel, scroll down to "Account Management" and click "Disconnect Slack". This removes the webhook configuration. You can reconnect at any time with a new webhook URL.

scanSections.question: What are Scan Sections?
scanSections.answer: Scan sections let you control which parts of your accessibility scan results are pushed to integrations. Available sections include: WCAG Compliance (WCAG violations), HTML Validation (HTML markup errors), Broken Links (link health issues), Accessibility Tree (screen reader compatibility), and Layout Issues (responsive design problems).

severityFilter.question: What is the Severity Filter?
severityFilter.answer: The severity filter lets you control which violations trigger integration actions based on their severity level: Critical (most severe accessibility barriers), Serious (significant issues), Moderate (medium-impact issues), and Minor (low-impact suggestions). You can enable or disable each severity level independently.

grouping.question: What are Grouping Options?
grouping.answer: Grouping options control how violations are organized: "Per Error Type" creates separate entries for each unique violation type, while "Single Issue" combines all violations into one entry. For GitHub and Slack, the "Single Issue" option may cause content truncation due to platform character limits. Notion has no such limits.

regroupViolations.question: What does "Group Violations by Type" mean?
regroupViolations.answer: When enabled, violations of the same rule type (e.g., multiple missing alt texts) are combined into a single entry. When disabled, each individual violation creates its own entry. This helps reduce notification noise when multiple similar issues are found.

stillHaveQuestions.title: Still have questions?
stillHaveQuestions.description: Can't find what you're looking for? Our support team is here to help.

affiliates.title: Affiliate Program
affiliates.subtitle: Join our exclusive affiliate program and earn while helping businesses become accessible

intro.title: Become a LumTrails Affiliate
intro.description: We're launching an exclusive affiliate program for ambitious students from top business schools. This is your opportunity to gain real-world SaaS sales experience while earning significant commissions.

positions.title: Open Positions
positions.description: We're currently recruiting two student affiliates:

germany.title: Germany
germany.description: One position for a student from a German business school

france.title: France
france.description: One position for a student from a French business school

mission.title: Your Mission
mission.description: As a LumTrails affiliate, you'll be responsible for acquiring new customers through our monthly and yearly subscription plans. You'll leverage your network, creativity, and sales skills to help businesses discover our accessibility solution.

compensation.title: Compensation
compensation.subtitle: Earn 40% commission on every sale
compensation.description: You'll receive 40% of the sale value for every customer you bring to LumTrails. Commissions apply to both monthly and yearly plans, paid out as freelance compensation.
compensation.durationDescription: Earn commissions on your referred customers for a full year. After 12 months, the contract can be renewed based on performance.
compensation.structureDescription: You'll be compensated as an independent freelancer, giving you flexibility and real entrepreneurial experience.

mentoring.title: Professional Mentoring
mentoring.description: Gain invaluable insights from experienced professionals
mentoring.sessionsDescription: You'll receive guidance from a senior SaaS sales professional who will help you develop your sales strategy, refine your pitch, and accelerate your success.

experience.title: Real Startup Experience
experience.description: Gain hands-on experience in the startup world, selling a B2B SaaS product to real customers.

skills.title: Develop Sales Skills
skills.description: Learn the art of consultative selling, objection handling, and closing deals in a professional environment.

network.title: Build Your Network
network.description: Connect with business decision-makers and expand your professional network across Europe.

flexibility.title: Flexible Schedule
flexibility.description: Work on your own terms while balancing your studies. Set your own hours and work remotely.

income.title: Significant Earning Potential
income.description: With 40% commissions on subscription plans, your earning potential is directly tied to your effort and results.

impact.title: Make an Impact
impact.description: Help businesses become accessible and compliant with the European Accessibility Act—a meaningful mission.

product.title: About LumTrails
product.description: LumTrails is a cutting-edge SaaS platform that helps businesses achieve compliance with the European Accessibility Act (EAA). Our cloud-native solution leverages a modern microservices architecture—including dedicated web and PDF scanning engines, automated report generation, and seamless integrations with Slack, Notion, and GitHub. Powered by AI-assisted workflows, LumTrails enables development teams to quickly identify WCAG violations, generate professional compliance reports, and track accessibility improvements over time. It's the perfect product for entrepreneurs passionate about tech, sustainability, and social impact.

apply.title: Ready to Apply?
apply.description: Interested in joining our affiliate program? Contact us with your CV and a brief introduction explaining why you'd be a great fit.
apply.note: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Early applications are encouraged.
